content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 12, 1991


Robert N. Joennck
Town Attorney
Town of Loomis
Post Office Box 1327
Loomis, CA  95650

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. G-91-352

Dear Mr. Joennck:

     You have requested advice under the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹

     The Act requires the Commission to provide formal written
advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or
to that person's authorized representative.  (Section 83114(b).)
The Commission does not provide advice to third parties concerning
another person's duties under the Act.  (Regulation 18329(b)(8),
copy enclosed.)  It does not appear that you have the
authorization of Mr. Ron Barringer or Mr. Stan Nader to request
advice on their behalf from the Commission.

     Advice may be given to persons, such as yourself, with a duty
to advise other persons relating to their duties or actions under
the Act or to an agency whose members or employees are subject to
the provisions of the Act.  (Regulation 18329(c)(1).)  However, it
is our understanding that you were requesting advice on behalf of
the Town of Loomis in your capacity as the town's city attorney,
and not on behalf of the Placer County Local Agency Formation
Commission ("LAFCO") or of any of its members.


¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to Regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. G-91-352
Page 2


     Finally, one of the issues raised in your letter appeared to
be related to past conduct of one of the board members of LAFCO.
The Commission also does not provide advice relating to past
conduct.2 (Regulation 18329(b)(8).).

     For all these reasons, we are unable to provide advice on the
issues you raised in your letter. However, as we discussed in our
telephone conversation of July 22, 1991, you may bring your
concerns to the attention of LAFCO's counsel who does have the
authority to request prospective advice regarding the duties and
conduct of LAFCO's board members under the Act.

     We have enclosed the following materials for your general
information: (1) A Guide to the Political Reform Act of 1974 -
California's Conflict of Interest Law for Public Officials, and
(2) A Guide to Government Code Section 84308 of the Political
Reform Act of 1974 - Conflicts of Interest and Campaign
Contributions.

     Should you receive appropriate authorization, or if the
public officials or LAFCO's counsel contact the Commission
directly, we will be happy to respond. If you have any further
questions regarding this matter, please feel free to contact me at
(916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        By: Deanne Stone
                                            Counsel, Legal Division

SH:DS:aa

Enclosures




_________________________________________________________________
2     You informed me during our telephone conversation of July 22,
1991 that you did not wish this letter to be lodged as a complaint
with the Enforcement Division of the Commission at this time.
